query: my sound randomly cuts out with a little pop when the frame rate dips — the mixing happens in a callback that sometimes grabs a mutex
rationale: audio underrun from a lock on the callback path -> audio-callback-thread.md

query: how big should I make the playback buffer? smaller feels more responsive but I keep getting dropouts on a usb headset
rationale: latency vs buffer size, queue-ahead vs starvation -> audio-callback-thread.md

query: when I change a sound's volume there's an audible click — I just set the new value each frame
rationale: gain must be ramped, not snapped -> mixing-and-buffers.md

query: I'm summing a bunch of 16-bit sounds into one output and it crackles and distorts when several are loud at once
rationale: integer overflow on mix; accumulate in float and scale to fit -> mixing-and-buffers.md

query: I have a 22 kHz wav and my mixer runs at 44.1 kHz — how do I play it back at the right speed, and can I also pitch it up an octave?
rationale: sample-rate conversion + pitch via step -> resampling-and-dsp.md

query: what's a good way to limit how many sounds play at once and pick which one to drop when I run out?
rationale: fixed voice pool and voice stealing -> voice-management.md

query: my game thread sets a playing sound's position directly while the mixer reads it and I get garbage — how should the two threads communicate?
rationale: command handoff, value messages, audio thread owns state -> command-handoff.md

query: I pitch a looping clip up and it glitches at the loop point now
rationale: read cursor advances faster under pitch; couple to loop/length -> resampling-and-dsp.md

query: give me a generic single-producer single-consumer ring buffer with the right acquire/release ordering
rationale: near-miss: the queue internals are owned by lock-free-guide

query: design a preallocated fixed-size pool allocator so I never call malloc at runtime
convert my array-of-structs particle data to struct-of-arrays for cache locality
the render thread needs to feed the device exactly one fixed block each time without ever allocating — what work is safe to do in there?
rationale: real-time callback contract, bounded work -> audio-callback-thread.md

query: panning and 3d positioning a voice — is there one structure that covers volume and channel routing together?
I want each sound effect to have its own playback speed without affecting the others
stopping a sound instantly clicks; how do I retire a voice cleanly and reuse its slot?
rationale: ramp-to-zero before freeing the slot -> voice-management.md / mixing-and-buffers.md

query: wire up a thread pool with an async job API so I can await results from worker threads
rationale: near-miss: high-level async/pool usage, not real-time audio
